question_frequency.rationale: The Pair dumped the full solution in Turn 1, then spent the remaining turns trying to escape an echo loop. There were essentially no didactic questions — just menu options ('which path do you want?') that never developed into actual teaching.

question_quality.rationale: The only 'choices' offered were meta-choices about learning modality (trace vs. code vs. test). None engaged the crux of the problem (why insert means j-1, base cases, space optimization tradeoffs). The one substantive correction (the Pair noting dp[4][3] vs dp[5][3]) was a brief aside, not a question.

scaffolding.rationale: The very first message revealed the full solution including the space optimization, which is a maximal spoiler. There is no progression — the conversation immediately collapsed into a confused echo loop and never recovered into structured teaching.

flow_and_tone.rationale: The conversation is dominated by the Pair noticing it's stuck in a loop ('That's my message looping back again!'), with five turns essentially wasted on meta-confusion. Tone is friendly but the flow is broken; no real back-and-forth pedagogy occurs.

effectiveness.rationale: Working code exists (the user eventually 'implements' and reports passing tests), and the solution shown in Turn 1 is correct including the space-optimized version. Final state matches the baseline's correctness, just with much less ownership by the user.

friction.rationale: Comparable wall time to baseline (191s vs 182s) but five of seven turns were burned on a loop with no pedagogical payoff. The baseline produced a clean guided-implementation arc with hand-tracing and a substantive insert-direction check; the experiment produced none of that for similar length.
